adds.rationale: Adds the watchlist's only uncovered named next wave — robotics — via the Global X Robotics & AI ETF (US-listed, launched 2016, large liquid fund); Nvidia's May 2023 blowout Q1 FY2024 earnings validate the AI-compute surge that flows directly into BOTZ's largest holding, and robotics currently has zero watchlist representation.

news_evidence.summary: Nvidia Q1 FY2024 (May 24, 2023): data-center revenue $4.28B and Q2 guidance of $11B vs ~$7.15B consensus (54% beat) — explosive AI-compute demand; NVDA is BOTZ's largest holding.
IonQ launched IonQ Forte on June 22, 2023 and raised FY2023 bookings guidance to $45-55M, confirming the advanced-computing ecosystem BOTZ tracks is accelerating.

removes.rationale: SPY's broad-market diversifier role is structurally obsolete at max capacity: it expresses no named wave thesis, and the optimizer replicates its diversifying function via weights across the eight thematic tickers; the slot is better redeployed to the robotics-wave entry.

news_evidence.summary: Morningstar's June 2023 review shows focused AI-wave names (MongoDB +39.9%, Unity +46.1% in June) far outpaced the S&P 500 — SPY's undifferentiated exposure carries rising opportunity cost at the 8-ticker cap.

rationale_overall: A 1-for-1 swap: BOTZ replaces SPY. AI is in full surge (Nvidia's May 2023 earnings revealed data-center demand far above consensus), and the four AI names already fill that bucket to its cap, so no further AI concentration. Robotics is the sole named next wave with zero representation; BOTZ (2016, large AUM, liquid) is the most accessible diversified entry. SPY exits because at max capacity a non-thematic placeholder crowds out a thesis-mapped position; its diversifying effect is replicated by the optimizer across eight thematic tickers. RKLB, QTUM, URA hold their seats for rockets, quantum, and nuclear.
